title: 2-Hydroxy-4 - Methylacetophenone Oxime (HMAOX) as an Analytical Reagent: Studies on Palladium Chelate
author: (KUM.) L. C. JHAVERI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemistry Department, M. R. Science Institute, Gujarat College, Ahmedabad-380006 

  Manuscript received 20 July 1976, revised 24 February 1977, accepted 24 November 1977  

 2-Hydroxy-4-methylacetophenone oxime (HMAOX) has been used as a reagent for gravimetric determination of palladium and for its separation from several cations and anions. Palladinm is quantitatively precipitated by HMAOX between  \(p\) H range 1.5-4.0. After drying the precipitate at 120°, its composition corresponds to the formula Pd(C 9 H 10 O 3 N) 2 . Interference due to copper(II) and titanium(IV) has been eliminated by masking with EDTA and NaF or KF respectively and in other cases by suitable control of  \(p\) H.
